scan-and-fix.prompt: Scan repos for open issues (bugs, feature requests, enhancements -- all types, not just bugs). First, discover new repos by running `find /q/src -maxdepth 4 -name ".git" -type d` and comparing against the known target repos table. For any new repo found: check if it has uncommitted changes on non-default branches (skip those); if clean, run `git pull` to sync. Then for each known target repo: (1) List all open issues. (2) For each open issue, check if a PR already exists: run `GH_TOKEN=<PAT> gh pr list --repo <owner>/<repo> --search "#<issue_number>" --state all --json number,state,mergedAt,url,title`. If a PR is found and merged, close the issue with reason 'completed' and comment 'Closed -- fixed by PR #N (merged).'. If a PR is found and closed without merge, close the issue with reason 'not planned' and comment 'Closing -- PR #N was closed. See PR for details.'. If a PR is still open, skip the issue. Only proceed to fix if no PR exists. (3) Skip issues already in the attempts log. (4) Pick the highest-priority remaining issue (bugs first, then enhancements, then unlabeled; oldest first within same type), analyze the root cause, implement a fix, and submit a PR. In the scan report, add a 'New Repos Detected' section listing any new repos with their path and a copyable CLI suggestion to add them. Follow the instructions in your agent file.
scan-and-fix.description: Scans repos for all open issues (bugs, enhancements, feature requests), discovers new repos, analyzes root cause, creates fix PRs

open-pr-maintenance.prompt: Maintain all open PRs I authored across target repos. For each repo: (1) List my open PRs with `gh pr list --author @me --state open`. (2) For each PR, check for merge conflicts -- if found, rebase or merge main, resolve conflicts preserving intent, push updated branch, comment that conflicts were resolved. (3) Check for unresolved review comments -- identify comments requesting changes or asking questions with no reply from me. For each: read the code, implement the requested change or answer the question, push fix, reply to the comment. Skip PRs with no conflicts and no unaddressed comments. Follow safety rules in your agent file. If NO PRs needed any maintenance (no conflicts resolved, no comments addressed), do NOT generate an HTML report -- just output 'No open PRs to maintain.' and exit. Only generate a report when actual work was done. When generating a report, you MUST use the fixed template at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/templates/bug-killer-pr-maintenance.html. Read the template file, then replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values with actual data. Do NOT redesign the layout, change colors, rearrange sections, or generate HTML from scratch. Replace {{VO_SUBTITLE}} with: Agent: Bug Killer | Job: open-pr-maintenance | Start: <start time> | Complete: <complete time>.
open-pr-maintenance.description: Maintain open PRs: resolve merge conflicts and address review comments

daily-summary.prompt: Generate a daily summary report for all bug-killer activity from the past 24 hours. Steps: (1) List all HTML files in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/ modified in the last 24 hours (exclude *-latest.html symlinks and the daily-summary files themselves). (2) For each file, extract from the HTML: the issue/PR number, repo name, title, outcome (fixed/skipped/failed), and the PR URL if one was created. (3) Also collect and link reports from ALL other bug-killer jobs (scan-and-fix, open-pr-maintenance) that ran in the past 24h -- the daily summary should be the single report to keep open, linking to all individual reports. Do NOT open individual reports in Edge. (4) You MUST use the fixed template at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/templates/bug-killer-daily-summary.html. Read the template file, then replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values with actual data. Do NOT redesign the layout, change colors, rearrange sections, or generate HTML from scratch. Replace {{VO_SUBTITLE}} with: Agent: Bug Killer | Job: daily-summary | Start: <ISO timestamp when job started> | Complete: <ISO timestamp when report is generated>. Replace {{ACTIVITY_TABLE_ROWS}} with one row per run report, where each row links to the individual run report as a file:/// URL. Columns: Time, Job Type (scan-and-fix / open-pr-maintenance), Repo, Issue/PR, Title, Outcome, Link to Report. If no activity in the past 24h, replace the table with the no-activity message. Replace {{JOB_DURATIONS_CONTENT}} by running: python Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/runner/Get-JobDurations.py --agent bug-killer and inserting its output. Save the report to Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/daily-summary-YYYYMMDD.html and also copy as daily-summary-latest.html. (5) Open ONLY the daily-summary report in Edge using a tab group: Start-Process msedge '--new-window --group-name=Daily "<report-path>"'
daily-summary.description: Daily summary linking all bug-killer activity reports from the past 24h, opened in Edge Daily tab group
